key_insight: The paper combines slice-wise misalignment statistics with autoregressive synthetic growth to generate 3D fiber microstructures that closely match X-ray-μCT observations.
engineering_relevance: This enables simulation-ready composite geometries for virtual testing, helping engineers study as-manufactured misalignment effects without relying solely on expensive experimental scans.
method: Extract per-slice and per-fiber in-plane/out-of-plane misalignment profiles using an analytical ellipse-intersection approach.
Fit a stochastic model with copula-based dependence, latent autoregression, and rare extreme-misalignment motifs.
Generate non-overlapping synthetic fibers via layer-wise seeding, Delaunay neighborhoods, and ellipse-based contact resolution.
evidence_signal: The optimized model reportedly matches the original statistical descriptors closely, with deviations generally below 10%, and generates about 2400 synthetic fibers.
watch_item: The workflow depends on careful hyperparameter calibration and physically consistent contact handling, so robustness may vary when transferred to different composite systems or scan qualities.
paper_title: Autoregressive Modelling and Synthetic Generation of High-Fidelity, Statistically Equivalent 3D Microstructures for As-Manufactured Misalignments in Fiber-Reinforced Composites
